UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information within the HealthLinke application. This includes personal details and preferences.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for communication and login (though authentication is external).

phoneNumber.description: The user's primary phone number, used for contact and notifications.

addressIds.description: References to the user's saved addresses. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Address)

languagePreference.description: The user's preferred language for the application (e.g., 'en', 'hi', 'hinglish').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Address.title: Address
Address.description: Represents a physical address associated with a user or pharmacy.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Address entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this address. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Address)

addressLine1.description: The first line of the street address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the street address (optional).

state.description: The state or region of the address.

postalCode.description: The postal code or zip code of the address.

isDefault.description: Indicates if this is the user's default address.

Doctor.title: Doctor
Doctor.description: Represents a verified doctor available for consultation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Doctor entity.

photoUrl.description: URL to the doctor's profile photo.

experienceYears.description: Number of years of experience the doctor has.

specializations.description: A list of medical specializations the doctor holds (e.g., 'Cardiology', 'Pediatrics').

consultationFee.description: The standard fee for a consultation with this doctor.

consultationTypes.description: Types of consultations offered by the doctor (e.g., 'Chat', 'Audio Call', 'Video Call').

averageRating.description: The average rating received by the doctor from patients.

totalReviews.description: The total number of reviews received by the doctor.

isVerified.description: Indicates if the doctor's credentials have been verified.

description.description: A brief biographical description or professional summary of the doctor.

Medicine.title: Medicine
Medicine.description: Represents a medicine product available in the online store.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Medicine entity.

name.description: The brand or trade name of the medicine.

genericName.description: The generic or chemical name of the medicine, used for suggestions.

description.description: A detailed description of the medicine, including uses, dosage, and warnings.

imageUrl.description: URL to the medicine's product image.

price.description: The base price of the medicine.

discountPercentage.description: The percentage discount applicable to the medicine.

categoryIds.description: References to categories this medicine belongs to (e.g., 'Tablets', 'Ayurvedic'). (Relationship: Medicine N:N Category)

isPrescriptionRequired.description: Indicates if a prescription is required to purchase this medicine.

stockQuantity.description: The current quantity of the medicine available in stock.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a categorization for various items like medicines, lab tests, or wellness content.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The name of the category (e.g., 'Tablets', 'Blood Test', 'Yoga Videos').

description.description: A description of what the category encompasses.

parentCategoryId.description: Optional reference to a parent category, enabling hierarchical categorization. (Relationship: Category 1:N Category - self-referencing)

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a user's order, which can include medicines, lab tests, or both.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who placed the order. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Order)

orderDate.description: The date and time when the order was placed.

deliveryAddressId.description: Reference to the Address where the order should be delivered or sample collected. (Relationship: Address 1:N Order)

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'Pending', 'Confirmed', 'Shipped', 'Delivered', 'Cancelled').

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order, including discounts and delivery charges.

paymentMethod.description: The method of payment used for the order (e.g., 'COD', 'Online').

paymentStatus.description: The current payment status of the order (e.g., 'Paid', 'Unpaid', 'Refunded').

trackingNumber.description: A unique tracking number for the delivery of the order.

estimatedDeliveryDate.description: The estimated date and time for order delivery.

prescriptionId.description: Optional reference to a Prescription associated with this order. (Relationship: Prescription 1:N Order)

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents an individual item within an Order, which can be a medicine or a lab test.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the parent Order. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

medicineId.description: Reference to the Medicine if this item is a medicine. (Relationship: Medicine 1:N OrderItem)

labTestId.description: Reference to the LabTest if this item is a lab test. (Relationship: LabTest 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the medicine or lab test booked.

unitPrice.description: The price of a single unit of the item at the time of order.

subtotal.description: The total price for this specific order item (quantity * unitPrice).

Prescription.title: Prescription
Prescription.description: Represents an uploaded prescription from a user or one generated during a doctor consultation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Prescription entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this prescription. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Prescription)

uploadDate.description: The date and time when the prescription was uploaded.

imageUrl.description: URL to the image or PDF of the prescription document.

doctorNote.description: Any notes added by a doctor or staff related to this prescription.

associatedOrderId.description: Optional reference to an Order that utilized this prescription. (Relationship: Order 1:N Prescription)

status.description: The processing status of the prescription (e.g., 'Pending Review', 'Approved', 'Rejected').

expirationDate.description: The expiration date of the prescription, if applicable.

LabTest.title: LabTest
LabTest.description: Defines a specific lab test offered by HealthLinke.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LabTest entity.

name.description: The name of the lab test (e.g., 'Blood Test', 'Sugar Test').

description.description: A detailed description of the lab test, its purpose, and what it measures.

price.description: The price of the lab test.

preparationInstructions.description: Instructions for the patient to prepare for the test (e.g., 'fasting required').

reportDeliveryTime.description: The estimated time for report delivery (e.g., '24 hours', '3 days').

homeSampleCollectionAvailable.description: Indicates if home sample collection is available for this test.

categoryIds.description: References to categories this lab test belongs to (e.g., 'Blood Tests', 'Full Body Checkup'). (Relationship: LabTest N:N Category)

LabTestBooking.title: LabTestBooking
LabTestBooking.description: Represents a specific instance of a user booking a lab test.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LabTestBooking entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who booked the test. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N LabTestBooking)

labTestId.description: Reference to the LabTest that was booked. (Relationship: LabTest 1:N LabTestBooking)

bookingDate.description: The date and time when the lab test was booked.

preferredCollectionDate.description: The user's preferred date and time for sample collection.

collectionAddressId.description: Reference to the Address for home sample collection. (Relationship: Address 1:N LabTestBooking)

status.description: The current status of the lab test booking (e.g., 'Booked', 'Sample Collected', 'Report Ready', 'Cancelled').

reportUrl.description: URL to the downloadable PDF report of the lab test results.

orderId.description: Optional reference to an Order if this booking is part of a larger order. (Relationship: Order 1:N LabTestBooking)

HealthRecord.title: HealthRecord
HealthRecord.description: Stores a historical health record entry for a user or family member, such as a prescription, test report, or doctor's note.

id.description: Unique identifier for the HealthRecord entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this health record belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N HealthRecord)

familyMemberId.description: Optional reference to a FamilyMember if this record belongs to one. (Relationship: FamilyMember 1:N HealthRecord)

recordDate.description: The date and time when this health record entry was created or pertains to.

type.description: The type of health record (e.g., 'Prescription', 'Lab Report', 'Doctor Note', 'Vaccination').

documentUrl.description: URL to the digital document (e.g., PDF, image) associated with this record.

notes.description: Free-form text notes related to this health record entry.

associatedEntityId.description: Reference to the original entity that generated this record (e.g., Prescription.id, LabTestBooking.id, DoctorConsultation.id).

associatedEntityType.description: The type of the associated entity (e.g., 'Prescription', 'LabTestBooking', 'DoctorConsultation').

FamilyMember.title: FamilyMember
FamilyMember.description: Represents a family member whose health profile is managed by a primary user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FamilyMember entity.

userId.description: Reference to the primary UserProfile managing this family member. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N FamilyMember)

firstName.description: The first name of the family member.

lastName.description: The last name of the family member.

relationship.description: The relationship of the family member to the primary user (e.g., 'Spouse', 'Child', 'Parent').

dateOfBirth.description: The date of birth of the family member.

gender.description: The gender of the family member.

DoctorConsultation.title: DoctorConsultation
DoctorConsultation.description: Records details of a consultation session between a user and a doctor.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DoctorConsultation entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who participated in the consultation. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N DoctorConsultation)

doctorId.description: Reference to the Doctor who provided the consultation. (Relationship: Doctor 1:N DoctorConsultation)

timeSlotId.description: Reference to the TimeSlot booked for this consultation. (Relationship: TimeSlot 1:N DoctorConsultation)

consultationDate.description: The date and time when the consultation occurred.

consultationType.description: The type of consultation (e.g., 'Chat', 'Audio Call', 'Video Call').

feesPaid.description: The amount of fees paid for this consultation.

isFollowUp.description: Indicates if this consultation is a follow-up session.

followUpFee.description: The fee paid for a follow-up consultation, if applicable.

consultationSummary.description: A summary or notes from the doctor about the consultation.

prescriptionId.description: Optional reference to a new Prescription issued during this consultation. (Relationship: Prescription 1:N DoctorConsultation)

recordingUrl.description: URL to the recording of the audio/video call, if applicable.

chatTranscriptUrl.description: URL to the transcript of the chat consultation, if applicable.

ratingGiven.description: The rating given by the user for this consultation (1-5).

reviewText.description: The review text provided by the user for this consultation.

TimeSlot.title: TimeSlot
TimeSlot.description: Represents an available time slot for a doctor's consultation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TimeSlot entity.

doctorId.description: Reference to the Doctor to whom this time slot belongs. (Relationship: Doctor 1:N TimeSlot)

startTime.description: The starting date and time of the available slot.

endTime.description: The ending date and time of the available slot.

isBooked.description: Indicates if this time slot has been booked by a user.

bookedByUserId.description: Optional reference to the UserProfile who booked this slot. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N TimeSlot)

consultationId.description: Optional reference to the DoctorConsultation that was created using this slot. (Relationship: DoctorConsultation 1:N TimeSlot)

HealthTip.title: HealthTip
HealthTip.description: Stores daily health tips or wellness advice for users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the HealthTip entity.

title.description: The title of the health tip.

content.description: The detailed content or advice of the health tip.

imageUrl.description: Optional URL to an image associated with the health tip.

publishDate.description: The date and time when the health tip was published.

category.description: The category of the health tip (e.g., 'Diet', 'Exercise', 'Mental Health').

Coupon.title: Coupon
Coupon.description: Represents promotional coupons or offers for medicines or services.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Coupon entity.

code.description: The unique code that users enter to apply the coupon.

description.description: A description of the coupon's offer.

discountType.description: The type of discount offered (e.g., 'Percentage', 'Fixed Amount').

discountValue.description: The value of the discount (e.g., '10' for 10% or '50' for ₹50 off).

minimumOrderAmount.description: The minimum order amount required to use this coupon, if any.

validFrom.description: The date and time from which the coupon is valid.

validUntil.description: The date and time until which the coupon is valid.

isActive.description: Indicates if the coupon is currently active and usable.

applicableToMedicineIds.description: References to specific Medicine IDs to which this coupon applies. (Relationship: Coupon N:N Medicine)

applicableToCategoryIds.description: References to specific Category IDs (e.g., for medicines or lab tests) to which this coupon applies. (Relationship: Coupon N:N Category)

Referral.title: Referral
Referral.description: Tracks a user referral event and its associated rewards.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Referral entity.

referrerUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who referred a new user. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Referral - as referrer)

referredUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who was referred. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Referral - as referred)

referralCodeUsed.description: The referral code used during the sign-up of the referred user.

rewardStatus.description: The status of the referral reward (e.g., 'Pending', 'Credited', 'Claimed').

referralDate.description: The date and time when the referral event occurred.

Pharmacy.title: Pharmacy
Pharmacy.description: Represents a partner pharmacy or franchise that fulfills medicine orders.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Pharmacy entity.

addressId.description: Reference to the physical Address of the pharmacy. (Relationship: Address 1:N Pharmacy)

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number for the pharmacy.

email.description: The contact email address for the pharmacy.

contactPerson.description: The name of the primary contact person at the pharmacy.

isActive.description: Indicates if the pharmacy is currently active and fulfilling orders.

partnerSince.description: The date and time when the pharmacy became a partner.

deliveryRadiusKm.description: The maximum delivery radius in kilometers from the pharmacy.

definition.description: Stores individual user profile information. The document ID (`{userId}`) matches the authenticated user's UID. Access is restricted to the owner (`request.auth.uid == userId`).
Stores physical addresses belonging to a specific user. The `userId` in the path ensures authorization independence. Includes denormalized 'userId' for explicit ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns this address.

params.name: addressId
params.description: The unique ID of the address.

definition.description: Stores information about verified doctors. Publicly readable by all authenticated users; write access restricted to administrators. Supports QAPs for listing doctors.

params.name: doctorId
params.description: The unique ID of the doctor.

definition.description: Catalog of available medicine products. Publicly readable by all authenticated users; write access restricted to administrators. Supports QAPs for listing medicines.

params.name: medicineId
params.description: The unique ID of the medicine.

definition.description: Defines categories for medicines, lab tests, or wellness content. Publicly readable by all authenticated users; write access restricted to administrators. Supports QAPs for listing categories.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique ID of the category.

definition.description: Stores user-specific orders. The `userId` in the path ensures authorization independence. Includes denormalized 'userId' for explicit ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who placed this order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique ID of the order.

definition.description: Represents individual items within a user's order. Inherits authorization from the parent Order. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'orderId' for authorization independence (though typically read in context of parent).

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the parent order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The ID of the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the order item.

definition.description: Stores uploaded or generated prescriptions for a specific user. The `userId` in the path ensures authorization independence. Includes denormalized 'userId' for explicit ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns this prescription.

params.name: prescriptionId
params.description: The unique ID of the prescription.

definition.description: Catalog of available lab tests. Publicly readable by all authenticated users; write access restricted to administrators. Supports QAPs for listing lab tests.

params.name: labTestId
params.description: The unique ID of the lab test.

definition.description: Records bookings for lab tests made by a specific user. The `userId` in the path ensures authorization independence. Includes denormalized 'userId' for explicit ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who booked this lab test.

params.name: bookingId
params.description: The unique ID of the lab test booking.

definition.description: Stores historical health records for a user or their family members. The `userId` in the path ensures authorization independence. Includes denormalized 'userId' for explicit ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns this health record.

params.name: recordId
params.description: The unique ID of the health record.

definition.description: Manages profiles for family members under a primary user. The `userId` in the path ensures authorization independence. Includes denormalized 'userId' for explicit ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the primary user managing this family member.

params.name: familyMemberId
params.description: The unique ID of the family member.

definition.description: Records details of doctor consultation sessions for a specific user. The `userId` in the path ensures authorization independence. Includes denormalized 'userId' for explicit ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who participated in this consultation.

params.name: consultationId
params.description: The unique ID of the doctor consultation.

definition.description: Stores available time slots for doctor consultations. Publicly readable for browsing; includes denormalized 'doctorId' for doctor management and 'bookedByUserId' for user booking/cancellation, ensuring authorization independence for updates.

params.name: timeSlotId
params.description: The unique ID of the time slot.

definition.description: Provides daily health tips and wellness advice. Publicly readable by all authenticated users; write access restricted to administrators. Supports QAPs for listing health tips.

params.name: tipId
params.description: The unique ID of the health tip.

definition.description: Manages promotional coupons and offers. Publicly readable by all authenticated users; write access restricted to administrators. Supports QAPs for listing coupons.

params.name: couponId
params.description: The unique ID of the coupon.

definition.description: Tracks user referral events and rewards. Includes denormalized 'referrerUserId' and 'referredUserId' for authorization independence, allowing both users to view their related referral entries. Supports QAPs for listing user-specific referrals.

params.name: referralId
params.description: The unique ID of the referral event.

definition.description: Lists partner pharmacies. Publicly readable by all authenticated users; write access restricted to administrators. Pharmacy address details are expected to be embedded or handled separately to avoid conflict with user-owned Address schema. Supports QAPs for listing pharmacies.

params.name: pharmacyId
params.description: The unique ID of the pharmacy.

definition.description: Database-backed access control for administrators. The existence of a document at this path signifies an administrative role for the `{userId}`. Used for Authorization Independence in rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user with admin privileges.

definition.description: Database-backed access control for verified doctors. The existence of a document at this path signifies a doctor role for the `{userId}`. Used for Authorization Independence in rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user with doctor privileges.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and DBAC to ensure a secure, scalable, and debuggable system for HealthLinke. Authorization Independence is primarily achieved through two mechanisms:
1.  **Path-Based Ownership and Denormalization:** For all user-specific data (e.g., UserProfile, Address, Order, Prescription, HealthRecord, FamilyMember, DoctorConsultation, LabTestBooking), the authenticated user's ID (`request.auth.uid`) is directly embedded as a wildcard in the path (e.g., `/users/{userId}/addresses/{addressId}`). This allows security rules to directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the `{userId}` wildcard, eliminating the need for `get()` calls to parent documents to determine ownership. This makes authorization checks atomic, performant, and simplifies rule debugging. The relevant `userId` is also explicitly stored within the document itself for data integrity and client-side querying.
2.  **Denormalization for Shared/Complex Access:** For entities that involve multiple users or roles (e.g., TimeSlot, Referral), all necessary authorization context (like `doctorId`, `bookedByUserId`, `referrerUserId`, `referredUserId`) is denormalized directly into the document. This allows security rules to verify access by comparing `request.auth.uid` with fields within the `resource.data` or `request.resource.data` object without requiring any `get()` operations, further supporting atomic writes and reads.

**Structural Segregation** is applied by categorizing collections based on their security posture:
*   **User-Owned Data:** All data solely owned and managed by an individual user (e.g., their addresses, orders, health records) is nested under `/users/{userId}/...`. This automatically scopes access to the authenticated user and ensures that only they can view or modify their personal data.
*   **Publicly Viewable Data:** Entities like `Doctor`, `Medicine`, `Category`, `LabTest`, `HealthTip`, `Coupon`, and `Pharmacy` are placed in top-level collections. These are generally read-accessible by all authenticated users and writable only by administrators (defined via DBAC roles). This segregation simplifies security rules for these collections to `allow read: if request.auth != null;` and `allow write: if isAdmin();`.
*   **Roles:** Admin and Doctor roles are managed using the Database-Backed Access Control (DBAC) pattern. Dedicated collections like `/roles_admin/{userId}` and `/roles_doctor/{userId}` exist. The presence of a document at these paths grants the user the corresponding role. This allows for simple, efficient rule functions like `isAdmin()` and `isDoctor()` that check for document existence, avoiding custom claims.

**QAPs (Queries Are Not Filters)** are supported by this structure:
*   For user-owned data, the path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/...`) intrinsically enforces QAPs. A client querying `/users/myUserId/orders` will only receive documents owned by `myUserId`, and the security rules will prevent access to `/users/anotherUser/orders` if `request.auth.uid != anotherUser`.
*   For globally accessible collections, `list` operations are inherently secure as all documents share the same read permission (all authenticated users). Filtering for specific categories or types is expected client-side. For `TimeSlot` and `Referral`, while read access might be broad, write/update rules are granular based on the denormalized `userId`s, and `list` operations requiring specific user context (e.g., 'my referrals') would use client-side `where` clauses, which are then verified by the security rules, ensuring only authorized results are returned if a read rule is conditional (as for `Referral`).

Overall, this design prioritizes clarity, security, and performance by leveraging Firestore's hierarchical nature and strategic denormalization, minimizing complex `get()` operations in security rules and promoting easy debugging.
